Group-A and Group-B in the Information Cadre of the Directorate General of Information and Public Relations.
Officers working at Indian Institute of Mass Communication (IIMC), New Delhi
Regarding sending for training on Government Public Relations organized
Administrative approval is being granted subject to the following conditions.
2.1 For the following cadre of officers of the Directorate General of Information and Public Relations:
The five-day training will be held at IIMC, New Delhi.
Cadre and number of officers to be nominated for training:
Stages and forms of training:
Overall, the training program should be more effective and interactive.
The training of 91 officers has been planned in four batches as follows.
The first batch will be held from July 13, 2026 to August 7, as planned in the IIMC letter.
The entire training is proposed for the period 2026. It has also been proposed
The financial budget and expenditure statement for the training are as follows: -
The training in all these four phases will involve officers from all the cadres together.

para_trans: Government Decision Number: Mawaz - 2026/216 Q. No.63 / Mawaz-2 (34-A)
The training fee per officer per day is ₹7,000 / - as per IIMC norms.
It has been decided that each batch will undergo 5 (five) days of training.
A) Cost of training per officer: ₹7,000 - 5 days = ₹35,000 / -
b) Inclusive: These expenses include training fees, breakfast, tea, lunch, and essentials.
Training material is included. Breakfast and Dinner
The cost is to be borne by the trainee officer concerned.
a) To the Officer Trainee concerned as per the Government Decisions issued from time to time.
Residential allowance and travel allowance will be admissible during the training.
The training curriculum:
The 5-day training, as per IIMC schedule, covers the following subjects:
It will be:
Media Management and Campaign Planning: Designing Integrated Communication Campaigns, Press
Conference planning and media coverage of live events.
Digital and Social Media: Social Media Analytics, Govt.
Effective use of social media for communication and marix assessment.
Modern technology: use of AI for creative, media research (theory,
with demonstrations).
Media Skills and Crisis Management: Virtual Labs for TV and Radio
Lab-practical, as well as emergency communication.
Officers / staff other than the officers / staff nominated for this training.

The establishment should be submitted to the branch. The certificate is registered by the establishment branch concerned.
